message: Add microsoft package with Windows Event Log → OCSF mapping (#142)

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,37 @@
+---
+description: Windows Error Reporting (EID 1001) → OCSF Process Activity (1007, Terminate)
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.process_activity"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 1
+ocsf.class_uid = 1007
+ocsf.activity_id = 2  // Terminate
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+// WER fires after a crash has been recorded; the process has already exited.
+ocsf.status_id = 2  // Failure
+ocsf.severity_id = 3  // Medium — Windows Event Log Level 2 (Error)
+ocsf.status_code = move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.param9  // exception code (e.g. c0000005)
+
+// param5  = faulting application name (short name, e.g. "payload.exe")
+// param6  = faulting application version
+// param12 = full path to the faulting application
+ocsf.process = {
+  path: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.param12,
+  file: {
+    type_id: 8,  // Executable File
+    version: (move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.param6).string(),
+  },
+}
+ocsf.process.name = ocsf.process.path.split("\\")[-1]
+ocsf.process.file.name = ocsf.process.name
+ocsf.process.file.path = ocsf.process.path
+
+
+// param5 = app name (redundant with process.name — dropped)
+// param7, param13 = faulting module name/path   \
+// param8          = faulting module version       | left in unmapped
+// param10         = exception offset             /
+// param1, param2, param3, param4, param11 = WER IDs, fault type, cab, report GUID — unmapped
+drop ocsf.unmapped.EventData.param5

@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+---
+description: Application Error (EID 1000) → OCSF Process Activity (1007, Terminate)
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.process_activity"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 1
+ocsf.class_uid = 1007
+ocsf.activity_id = 2  // Terminate
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+ocsf.status_id = 2  // Failure
+ocsf.severity_id = 3  // Medium — Windows Event Log Level 2 (Error)
+ocsf.status_code = move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.param7  // exception code (e.g. c0000005 = access violation)
+
+// param9 = faulting process ID (hex string, e.g. "0x1a4c")
+// param11 = full path to the faulting application
+// param2  = faulting application version
+ocsf.process = {
+  pid: int(move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.param9, base=16),
+  path: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.param11,
+  file: {
+    type_id: 8,  // Executable File
+    version: (move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.param2).string(),
+  },
+}
+ocsf.process.name = ocsf.process.path.split("\\")[-1]
+ocsf.process.file.name = ocsf.process.name
+ocsf.process.file.path = ocsf.process.path
+
+// param1  = faulting application name (redundant with process.name — dropped)
+// param4  = faulting module name        \
+// param5  = faulting module version      | left in unmapped; no OCSF module object
+// param12 = faulting module path        /
+// param3, param6, param8, param10, param13 = timestamps, offset, report ID — unmapped
+drop ocsf.unmapped.EventData.param1

@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+---
+description: Application Hang (EID 1002) → OCSF Process Activity (1007, Terminate)
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.process_activity"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 1
+ocsf.class_uid = 1007
+ocsf.activity_id = 2  // Terminate
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+ocsf.status_id = 2  // Failure
+ocsf.severity_id = 3  // Medium — Windows Event Log Level 2 (Error)
+
+// param4 = faulting process ID (hex string, e.g. "0x1a4c")
+// param9 = full path to the hanging application
+// param2 = hanging application version
+ocsf.process = {
+  pid: int(move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.param4, base=16),
+  path: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.param9,
+  file: {
+    type_id: 8,  // Executable File
+    version: (move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.param2).string(),
+  },
+}
+ocsf.process.name = ocsf.process.path.split("\\")[-1]
+ocsf.process.file.name = ocsf.process.name
+ocsf.process.file.path = ocsf.process.path
+
+// param1 = application name (redundant with process.name — dropped)
+// param3 = application timestamp              \
+// param5 = process start time                  | left in unmapped; no OCSF target
+// param6 = hang duration (ms)                  |
+// param7 = hang flags                          |
+// param8 = WER report ID                      /
+drop ocsf.unmapped.EventData.param1

@@ -0,0 +1,50 @@
+---
+description: Logon with Explicit Credentials (EID 4648) → OCSF Authentication (3002, Logon)
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.authentication"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 3
+ocsf.class_uid = 3002
+ocsf.activity_id = 1  // Logon
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+// EID 4648 records the attempt, not the outcome; no status is available.
+
+ocsf.user = {
+  name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.TargetUserName,
+  domain: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.TargetDomainName,
+}
+
+// The process that called LogonUser/CreateProcessWithLogonW.
+ocsf.actor = {
+  user: {
+    uid: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectUserSid,
+    name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectUserName,
+    domain: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectDomainName,
+  },
+  session: {
+    uid: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.LogonGuid,
+    uid_alt: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectLogonId,
+  },
+  process: {
+    pid: int(move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.ProcessId, base=16),
+    path: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.ProcessName,
+    file: { type_id: 8 },
+  },
+}
+ocsf.actor.process.name = ocsf.actor.process.path.split("\\")[-1]
+ocsf.actor.process.file.name = ocsf.actor.process.name
+ocsf.actor.process.file.path = ocsf.actor.process.path
+
+ocsf.dst_endpoint = {
+  hostname: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.TargetServerName,
+}
+
+// Where the logon originated from (may differ from the actor's host in lateral movement).
+ocsf.src_endpoint = {
+  ip: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.IpAddress,
+  port: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.IpPort,
+}
+
+// TargetLogonGuid and TargetInfo stay in unmapped; no clean OCSF mapping.

@@ -0,0 +1,27 @@
+---
+description: Kerberos Pre-Authentication Failed (EID 4771) → OCSF Authentication (3002, Authentication Ticket/Failure)
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.authentication"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 3
+ocsf.class_uid = 3002
+ocsf.activity_id = 3  // Authentication Ticket (TGT request)
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+ocsf.status_id = 2  // Failure — pre-auth always fails for this EID
+ocsf.severity_id = 2  // Low
+ocsf.status_code = move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.Status  // Kerberos error code (e.g. 0x18 = wrong password)
+
+ocsf.user = {
+  uid: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.TargetSid,
+  name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.TargetUserName,
+}
+
+ocsf.src_endpoint = {
+  ip: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.IpAddress,
+  port: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.IpPort,
+}
+
+// PreAuthType, TicketOptions, ServiceName — unmapped.
+// CertIssuerName / CertSerialNumber / CertThumbprint (PKINIT) — unmapped.

@@ -0,0 +1,39 @@
+---
+description: Kerberos Service Ticket Requested (EID 4769) → OCSF Authentication (3002, Service Ticket)
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.authentication"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 3
+ocsf.class_uid = 3002
+ocsf.activity_id = 4  // Service Ticket
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+// Status 0x0 = success; anything else = failure (e.g. 0x1F = no TGT).
+ocsf.status_id = 1 if ocsf.unmapped.EventData.Status == "0x0" else 2
+ocsf.status_code = move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.Status
+
+// TargetUserName usually includes @REALM (e.g. "jdoe@CORP.LOCAL"); split off
+// the realm into domain. Fall back to TargetDomainName when no @ is present.
+_parts = (move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.TargetUserName).split("@")
+ocsf.user = {
+  name: _parts[0],
+  domain: _parts[1]? else move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.TargetDomainName,
+}
+drop _parts
+
+ocsf.dst_endpoint = {
+  svc_name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.ServiceName,
+}
+
+ocsf.src_endpoint = {
+  ip: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.IpAddress,
+  port: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.IpPort,
+}
+
+// The logon GUID links this TGS request back to the original TGT logon event.
+ocsf.session = {
+  uid: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.LogonGuid,
+}
+
+// TicketOptions, TicketEncryptionType, ServiceSid, TransmittedServices — unmapped.

@@ -0,0 +1,47 @@
+---
+description: Successful Logon (EID 4624) → OCSF Authentication (3002, Logon)
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.authentication"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 3
+ocsf.class_uid = 3002
+ocsf.activity_id = 1  // Logon
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+ocsf.status_id = 1  // Success
+
+// Windows LogonType integers map directly to OCSF logon_type_id values
+// (Interactive=2, Network=3, Batch=4, Service=5, Unlock=7, NetworkCleartext=8,
+//  NewCredentials=9, RemoteInteractive=10, CachedInteractive=11, …).
+ocsf.logon_type_id = move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.LogonType
+
+ocsf.user = {
+  uid: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.TargetUserSid,
+  name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.TargetUserName,
+  domain: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.TargetDomainName,
+}
+
+ocsf.actor = {
+  user: {
+    uid: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectUserSid,
+    name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectUserName,
+    domain: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectDomainName,
+  },
+  session: {
+    uid_alt: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectLogonId,
+  },
+}
+
+ocsf.src_endpoint = {
+  ip: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.IpAddress,
+  port: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.IpPort,
+  hostname: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.WorkstationName,
+}
+
+ocsf.auth_protocol = move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.AuthenticationPackageName
+
+ocsf.session = {
+  uid: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.LogonGuid,
+  uid_alt: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.TargetLogonId,
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,42 @@
+---
+description: Failed Logon (EID 4625) → OCSF Authentication (3002, Logon/Failure)
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.authentication"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 3
+ocsf.class_uid = 3002
+ocsf.activity_id = 1  // Logon
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+ocsf.status_id = 2  // Failure
+ocsf.severity_id = 2  // Low — single failed logon; brute-force detection is a higher layer
+ocsf.status_code = move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.Status  // hex NTSTATUS (e.g. 0xC000006D = wrong credentials)
+
+// Windows LogonType integers map directly to OCSF logon_type_id values.
+ocsf.logon_type_id = move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.LogonType
+
+ocsf.user = {
+  uid: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.TargetUserSid,
+  name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.TargetUserName,
+  domain: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.TargetDomainName,
+}
+
+ocsf.actor = {
+  user: {
+    uid: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectUserSid,
+    name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectUserName,
+    domain: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectDomainName,
+  },
+  session: {
+    uid_alt: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectLogonId,
+  },
+}
+
+ocsf.src_endpoint = {
+  ip: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.IpAddress,
+  port: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.IpPort,
+  hostname: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.WorkstationName,
+}
+
+ocsf.auth_protocol = move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.AuthenticationPackageName

@@ -0,0 +1,31 @@
+---
+description: NTLM Authentication (EID 4776) → OCSF Authentication (3002, Logon)
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.authentication"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 3
+ocsf.class_uid = 3002
+ocsf.activity_id = 1  // Logon
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+// Status 0x0 = success; non-zero = failure (e.g. 0xC000006A = wrong password).
+ocsf.status_id = 1 if ocsf.unmapped.EventData.Status == "0x0" else 2
+ocsf.status_code = move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.Status
+
+ocsf.user = {
+  name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.TargetUserName,
+}
+
+// PackageName identifies the NTLM variant; map to auth_protocol_id so that
+// ocsf::derive can populate the sibling auth_protocol string.
+let $protocols = {
+  "MICROSOFT_AUTHENTICATION_PACKAGE_V1_0": 1,  // NTLM v1
+  "NTLM": 1,
+}
+ocsf.auth_protocol_id = $protocols[ocsf.unmapped.EventData.PackageName]? else 99
+drop ocsf.unmapped.EventData.PackageName
+
+ocsf.src_endpoint = {
+  hostname: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.Workstation,
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,47 @@
+---
+description: Process Creation (EID 4688) → OCSF Process Activity (1007, Launch)
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.process_activity"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 1
+ocsf.class_uid = 1007
+ocsf.activity_id = 1  // Launch
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+ocsf.launch_type_id = 1  // Spawn
+
+ocsf.actor = {
+  user: {
+    uid: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectUserSid,
+    name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectUserName,
+    domain: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectDomainName,
+  },
+  session: {
+    uid_alt: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectLogonId,
+  },
+}
+
+ocsf.process = {
+  pid: int(move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.NewProcessId, base=16),
+  path: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.NewProcessName,
+  cmd_line: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.CommandLine,
+  file: { type_id: 8 },
+  parent_process: {
+    pid: int(move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.ProcessId, base=16),
+    path: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.ParentProcessName,
+    file: { type_id: 8 },
+  },
+}
+ocsf.process.name = ocsf.process.path.split("\\")[-1]
+ocsf.process.file.name = ocsf.process.name
+ocsf.process.file.path = ocsf.process.path
+ocsf.process.parent_process.name = ocsf.process.parent_process.path.split("\\")[-1]
+ocsf.process.parent_process.file.name = ocsf.process.parent_process.name
+ocsf.process.parent_process.file.path = ocsf.process.parent_process.path
+
+// EID 4688 only fires on success.
+ocsf.status_id = 1
+
+// TargetUserSid/Name/Domain are the impersonation target (usually null after
+// sentinel replacement). TokenElevationType and MandatoryLabel have no OCSF home.

@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@
+---
+description: Scheduled Task Created (EID 4698) → OCSF Scheduled Job Activity (1006, Create)
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.scheduled_job_activity"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 1
+ocsf.class_uid = 1006
+ocsf.activity_id = 1  // Create
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+// The process that made the Task Scheduler RPC call.
+ocsf.actor = {
+  user: {
+    uid: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectUserSid,
+    name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectUserName,
+    domain: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectDomainName,
+  },
+  session: {
+    uid_alt: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectLogonId,
+  },
+  process: {
+    pid: int(move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.ClientProcessId, base=16),
+  },
+}
+
+ocsf.job = {
+  name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.TaskName,
+}
+
+// TaskContent is an XML string describing triggers and actions. It is
+// intentionally left in unmapped — parsing it would require an XML operator
+// and the content is already preserved verbatim in raw_data.
+// ClientProcessStartKey, ParentProcessId, RpcCallClientLocality, FQDN also
+// remain in unmapped.

@@ -0,0 +1,20 @@
+---
+description: Service Crashed Unexpectedly (EID 7034) → OCSF Windows Service Activity (201004, Stop/Failure)
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.windows_service_activity"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 1
+ocsf.class_uid = 201004
+ocsf.activity_id = 4  // Stop
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+ocsf.status_id = 2  // Failure

@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+---
+description: Service Installed via Security Audit (EID 4697) → OCSF Windows Service Activity (201004, Create)
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.windows_service_activity"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 1
+ocsf.class_uid = 201004
+ocsf.activity_id = 1  // Create
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+ocsf.actor = {
+  user: {
+    uid: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectUserSid,
+    name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectUserName,
+    domain: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectDomainName,
+  },
+  session: {
+    uid_alt: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.SubjectLogonId,
+  },
+}
+
+ocsf.win_service = {
+  name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.ServiceName,
+  service_file: {
+    path: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.ServiceFileName,
+    type_id: 8,
+  },
+}
+// Strip quotes and arguments: "C:\path\svc.exe" --args → svc.exe
+ocsf.win_service.service_file.name = ocsf.win_service.service_file.path.split("\\")[-1].split(" ")[0].replace("\"", "")
+ocsf.win_service.service_start_name = move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.ServiceAccount
+
+// ServiceType (hex, e.g. "0x10") and ServiceStartType (integer, e.g. "2") are
+// left in unmapped — OCSF win_service enums exist but differ in encoding from
+// both the hex and integer Windows representations used here.

@@ -0,0 +1,26 @@
+---
+description: New Service Installed via SCM (EID 7045) → OCSF Windows Service Activity (201004, Create)
+---
+
+@name = "ocsf.windows_service_activity"
+
+ocsf.category_uid = 1
+ocsf.class_uid = 201004
+ocsf.activity_id = 1  // Create
+ocsf.type_uid = ocsf.class_uid * 100 + ocsf.activity_id
+
+ocsf.win_service = {
+  name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.ServiceName,
+  service_file: {
+    path: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.ImagePath,
+    type_id: 8,
+  },
+  // service_start_name is the Windows account the service runs as (e.g. LocalSystem).
+  service_start_name: move ocsf.unmapped.EventData.AccountName,
+}
+// Strip quotes and arguments: "C:\path\svc.exe" --args → svc.exe
+ocsf.win_service.service_file.name = ocsf.win_service.service_file.path.split("\\")[-1].split(" ")[0].replace("\"", "")
+
+// ServiceType and StartType are descriptive strings on this event (e.g.
+// "Own Process", "Auto Start"); numeric enums are on EID 4697. Leave them in
+// unmapped so downstream consumers can use whichever representation they prefer.

+---
+description: Microsoft Windows Event Log → OCSF
+args:
+  positional:
+    - name: log
+      description: The field that holds the raw Windows Event Log event as XML.
+      type: field
+---
+
+ocsf = {}
+
+ocsf.raw_data = $log
+ocsf.raw_data_size = $log.length_bytes()
+
+ocsf.unmapped = $log.parse_winlog()
+
+ocsf.metadata = {
+  event_code: (move ocsf.unmapped.System.EventID).string(),
+  extensions: [{name: "win"}],
+  log_format: "xml",
+  log_name: move ocsf.unmapped.System.Channel,
+  log_level: (move ocsf.unmapped.System.Level).string(),
+  log_version: (move ocsf.unmapped.System.Version).string(),
+  logged_time: move ocsf.unmapped.System.TimeCreated.SystemTime,
+  original_event_uid: (move ocsf.unmapped.System.EventRecordID).string(),
+  processed_time: now(),
+  product: {
+    name: move ocsf.unmapped.System.Provider.Name,
+    uid: move ocsf.unmapped.System.Provider.Guid?,
+    vendor_name: "Microsoft",
+  },
+  profiles: ["host"],
+  version: "1.7.0",
+}
+drop ocsf.unmapped.System.Provider
+drop ocsf.unmapped.System.TimeCreated
+
+
+ocsf.severity_id = 1
+
+// Native Windows events have only one timestamp (SystemTime); copy it to time.
+ocsf.time = ocsf.metadata.logged_time
+
+ocsf.device = {
+  hostname: move ocsf.unmapped.System.Computer,
+}
+
+// "-" is the universal Windows sentinel for "not applicable/empty".
+replace what="-", with=null
+
+if ocsf.metadata.event_code == "1000" {
+  microsoft::windows::ocsf::events::application_error
+} else if ocsf.metadata.event_code == "1001" {
+  microsoft::windows::ocsf::events::application_crash_report
+} else if ocsf.metadata.event_code == "1002" {
+  microsoft::windows::ocsf::events::application_hang
+} else if ocsf.metadata.event_code == "4624" {
+  microsoft::windows::ocsf::events::logon
+} else if ocsf.metadata.event_code == "4625" {
+  microsoft::windows::ocsf::events::logon_failed
+} else if ocsf.metadata.event_code == "4648" {
+  microsoft::windows::ocsf::events::explicit_credential_logon
+} else if ocsf.metadata.event_code == "4688" {
+  microsoft::windows::ocsf::events::process_create
+} else if ocsf.metadata.event_code == "4697" {
+  microsoft::windows::ocsf::events::service_install
+} else if ocsf.metadata.event_code == "4698" {
+  microsoft::windows::ocsf::events::scheduled_task_create
+} else if ocsf.metadata.event_code == "4720" {
+  microsoft::windows::ocsf::events::user_account_create
+} else if ocsf.metadata.event_code == "4732" {
+  microsoft::windows::ocsf::events::group_member_add
+} else if ocsf.metadata.event_code == "4769" {
+  microsoft::windows::ocsf::events::kerberos_service_ticket
+} else if ocsf.metadata.event_code == "4771" {
+  microsoft::windows::ocsf::events::kerberos_preauth_failed
+} else if ocsf.metadata.event_code == "4776" {
+  microsoft::windows::ocsf::events::ntlm_auth
+} else if ocsf.metadata.event_code == "6005" {
+  microsoft::windows::ocsf::events::eventlog_start
+} else if ocsf.metadata.event_code == "6006" {
+  microsoft::windows::ocsf::events::eventlog_stop
+} else if ocsf.metadata.event_code == "7034" {
+  microsoft::windows::ocsf::events::service_crashed
+} else if ocsf.metadata.event_code == "7045" {
+  microsoft::windows::ocsf::events::service_install_scm
+} else {
+  // Base Event — unknown/unsupported Windows EID
+  ocsf.category_uid = 0
+  ocsf.class_uid = 0
+  ocsf.activity_id = 0
+  ocsf.type_uid = 0
+}
